codeReview.summary: The worker replaced the auth placeholder and significantly expanded the tenant/admin section in .factory/library/frontend-handoff.md (337 insertions, 125 deletions). The documentation comprehensively covers all auth endpoints (AuthController, MfaController, UserProfileController), all superadmin tenant management endpoints (SuperAdminController, SuperAdminTenantOnboardingController), admin user management (AdminUserController), role management (RoleController), and the companies listing/control-plane surface (CompanyController). Auth flows (login with/without MFA, password reset, token refresh, MFA enrollment), JWT handling, error codes, DTOs, state machines, and UI hints are all thoroughly documented. The feature requirements (VAL-DOC-001, VAL-DOC-002) are fulfilled. One non-blocking gap: the AdminSettingsController endpoints (/api/v1/admin/settings, /tenant-runtime/metrics, /tenant-runtime/policy, /notify, /approvals) and several CompanyController support endpoints (admin-password-reset, support/warnings, tenant-metrics, tenant-runtime/policy at company-level) are not documented. These are secondary admin/support endpoints not explicitly listed in the feature description but a frontend developer building a full admin panel would need them.

issues.description: AdminSettingsController endpoints are undocumented: GET /api/v1/admin/settings, PUT /api/v1/admin/settings (SystemSettingsDto), GET /api/v1/admin/tenant-runtime/metrics, PUT /api/v1/admin/tenant-runtime/policy, POST /api/v1/admin/notify, GET /api/v1/admin/approvals. These admin panel endpoints would be needed by a frontend building the full admin experience.

CompanyController has additional superadmin-level endpoints not documented in the handoff: GET /api/v1/companies/superadmin/dashboard (CompanySuperAdminDashboardDto), POST /api/v1/companies/superadmin/tenants, PUT /api/v1/companies/superadmin/tenants/{id}, POST /api/v1/companies/{id}/lifecycle-state, GET /api/v1/companies/{id}/tenant-metrics, PUT /api/v1/companies/{id}/tenant-runtime/policy, POST /api/v1/companies/{id}/support/admin-password-reset, POST /api/v1/companies/{id}/support/warnings. Some overlap with SuperAdminController paths but these are distinct endpoints.

sharedStateObservations.observation: There are duplicate/overlapping endpoint paths for tenant management: SuperAdminController at /api/v1/superadmin/tenants/* AND CompanyController at /api/v1/companies/superadmin/tenants/* and /api/v1/companies/{id}/lifecycle-state. Both expose similar tenant lifecycle and creation functionality. This dual-path pattern is not documented in AGENTS.md and could confuse frontend developers. The handoff only documents the /api/v1/superadmin/* paths.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: CompanyController.java lines 56-84 (superadmin/dashboard, superadmin/tenants, /{id}/lifecycle-state) vs SuperAdminController.java lines 47-87 (dashboard, tenants, suspend/activate/deactivate, lifecycle-state)

sharedStateObservations.observation: The AdminSettingsController exposes system settings, tenant runtime metrics/policy, notification, and approval endpoints under /api/v1/admin/* that are part of the admin module but were not included in the frontend handoff. A future documentation pass should cover these for full admin panel frontend coverage.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: AdminSettingsController.java: GET/PUT /settings, GET /tenant-runtime/metrics, PUT /tenant-runtime/policy, POST /notify, GET /approvals

summary: Pass. The auth and tenant/admin frontend handoff documentation is comprehensive and fulfills VAL-DOC-001 and VAL-DOC-002. All primary auth endpoints (login, logout, refresh, MFA, password management, profile), tenant management endpoints (superadmin dashboard, onboarding, lifecycle, module gating), and admin user/role management endpoints are documented with complete flows, DTOs, error codes, state machines, and UI hints. Two non-blocking gaps noted: AdminSettingsController endpoints and some CompanyController support endpoints are not documented, but these fall outside the explicit feature scope.
